prompt: The memoized method still recomputes every call. What is the bug?
choices: HashMap is not thread-safe and corrupts the cache under recursion
If cache were declared as a local variable inside fib(), it would reset on every top-level call, defeating memoization
cache.get(n) returns a Long but the method returns long, causing unboxing NullPointerException
The base case n <= 1 must also be stored in the cache
explanation: A memoization cache must be shared across all recursive calls. Declaring it as a local variable inside fib() creates a fresh empty map on every invocation, so no previously computed values are ever found. Fix: declare cache as a static field (class level), which is what the outer code shows — the bug is the anti-pattern of putting it inside the method.
